title: Team-wide update on strategy pivot
description: Leadership is pivoting from horizontal to vertical SaaS. Write the team-wide update that explains the why, acknowledges the disruption, and rallies the team.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at ClinicDesk, a Series B vertical SaaS company (154 employees, $12.7M ARR). Your immediate assignment is team-wide update on strategy pivot. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Treat credibility with customers as a hard constraint, not a soft preference.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 39064
New logo adds: 881
Activation rate: 54%
30-day retention: 27%
NPS: -3 (n=71)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 11%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: 9 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: 3 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Jules Lopez): "Buyers are asking for proof points, not intent. We need visible traction fast."
VP Engineering (Derek Foster): "Unresolved platform dependencies make estimates look precise but wrong."
Head of Customer Success (Ishan Brooks): "Trust loss shows up before churn in expansion conversations."
Commercial note: 2 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 12 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Maple Home Goods (Operations Lead): "I can defend rollout if the first-week experience is consistent across teams."
Arbor Payments (IT Manager): "Metric definitions changed between reports, which reduced confidence in decision meetings."
Northgate Logistics (Director of Product): "I would expand if reporting and error states were easier for non-technical users."
Data gap: limited qualitative input from accounts that never activated.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 43
Median cycle time: 23 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 75%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 9
Release rollback events last quarter: 0
Average time to resolve escalations: 52 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: Map each audience to the message they need and why.
submissionFields.label: Audience and Intent

submissionFields.hint: Write the core narrative that aligns stakeholders on action.
submissionFields.label: Core Narrative

submissionFields.hint: Predict objections from key stakeholders and how you will respond.
submissionFields.label: Expected Pushback

submissionFields.hint: Draft the memo or all-hands update you would send.
submissionFields.label: Message Draft

submissionFields.hint: Explain how you will collect feedback and close open issues.
submissionFields.label: Follow-up and Feedback Loop

rubric.name: Audience Fit
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is messaging adapted to the needs and incentives of each audience?

rubric.description: Is communication clear, direct, and decision-oriented?

Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are claims grounded in evidence with transparent caveats?

expertSolution.content: Executives need decision logic and downside exposure, delivery teams need concrete scope and ownership, and customer-facing teams need timing and expectation framing. Tailor message depth by audience without changing the core narrative.

Recommendation: align on a staged plan because the data is mixed, not broken. Activation is 54% and retention is 27%, while NPS (-3, n=71) and 12 escalations indicate trust risk if execution quality slips.

Expect commercial pressure from Jules Lopez around the 2 deal pipeline, technical caution from Derek Foster on dependency risk (9 unresolved), and customer-impact pressure from Ishan Brooks due to escalation volume. Address each with explicit thresholds and review dates.

Team, we are moving to a bounded plan that prioritizes reliability and measurable customer value. Baseline today is 39064 active accounts, activation 54%, retention 27%, and on-time delivery 75%. We will publish weekly progress and revisit scope at the midpoint checkpoint.

Hold a structured Q&A within 24 hours, capture unresolved questions in a decision log, and assign owner plus due date for each item. Publish weekly updates that close the loop on objections raised by Sales, Engineering, and Customer Success.
